topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the open-plan office seemed to buzz with malicious intent. Sarah stared at the screen, her jaw tight enough to crack a tooth. The quarterly bonus breakdown was laid out, and the numbers, stark and unforgiving, pointed to a glaring disparity. Mark, the junior analyst, had secured a salary that dwarfed hers by a noticeable margin. A slow, hot flush crept up Sarah's neck. She gripped her mouse so hard her knuckles turned white, meticulously re-reading the pay structure, searching for an error, a mistake, anything to justify the imbalance. When Mark walked past her desk, whistling a cheerful tune, Sarah managed a clipped, overly bright smile that felt brittle on her face. "Great work on the projections, Mark," she said, the words tasting like ash in her mouth.

[story 2]

I felt a sudden, sharp tightening in my chest, like a fist squeezing my lungs. I had spent the last hour cross-referencing spreadsheets, the data swimming before my eyes, leading to one undeniable conclusion: Liam was earning significantly more than I was. A low, guttural sound escaped my throat, quickly suppressed by a forced clearing of my throat. I leaned back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning under my weight, and stared blankly at the beige wall. Every minute detail of Liam’s compensation package felt like a personal affront. When Liam approached, asking a simple question about a shared file, I kept my gaze fixed just above his shoulder, my posture rigid, every muscle coiled and ready to snap. "Of course, Liam," I managed, my voice unnaturally level, the forced calm barely masking the churning resentment inside me.

[story 3]

The air in the breakroom felt thick and suffocating. Maya held the printout, her fingers trembling slightly as she traced the figures showing the difference in annual earnings. It wasn't just a difference; it was a chasm. A deep, simmering heat began to radiate from her core, making the hairs on her arms prickle. She wanted to slam the paper down, to shout about unfairness, but the sheer awkwardness of the moment held her captive. When David, a senior manager, walked in, Maya quickly tucked the document into her bag, smoothing down her blouse with meticulous, almost aggressive movements. She offered a tight, professional nod, her eyes fixed on a point just over David’s shoulder, radiating a palpable sense of being deeply wronged without uttering a single word.
